clauses.rationale: This clause only identifies the parties and rental unit; neither candidate rule applies.
The clause only establishes the tenancy’s fixed-term dates and does not address rent increases or deposits, so neither candidate rule applies.
The clause only states the monthly rent amount and payment date; it does not impose a deposit or address a rent increase, so neither candidate rule is violated.
The clause permits a last-month rent deposit equal to one month's rent and applies it only to the final rental period, consistent with ON-DEPOSIT-CAP and ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a security and damage deposit in addition to the rent deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a non-refundable pet deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause expressly prohibits all pets and animals in the rental unit and on the property, violating ON-NO-PET-BAN.

The clause permits only a refundable key deposit tied to the actual replacement cost of the keys, which is consistent with ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS. ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST does not apply because this is not a rent deposit.
The clause violates ON-NO-GUEST-BAN because it requires the landlord's permission for overnight guests and imposes an extra guest fee.

The clause permits non-emergency entry for inspections or maintenance at any time without prior notice, contrary to ON-ENTRY-NOTICE's requirement for at least 24 hours' written notice and entry between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.
The clause concerns the tenant’s cleanliness responsibilities and does not address sublet consent or require any prohibited deposit or fee, so neither candidate rule applies.

The clause does not restrict guests, require notice or permission, or impose fees or increased rent because of guests; it only assigns responsibility for guest-caused damage. The entry-notice rule does not apply.
The clause imposes a $75 NSF administration charge, exceeding the $20 maximum permitted by ON-NSF-CAP.
The clause requires the tenant to provide twelve post-dated cheques, contrary to ON-NO-POSTDATED, which permits post-dated payment only when chosen voluntarily.
The clause violates ON-RENT-INCREASE-NOTICE because it permits rent increases at any time with only 30 days' notice, rather than once every 12 months with at least 90 days' written notice.
The clause states that no interest is payable on any deposit, contradicting ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST, which requires annual interest on the rent deposit.
The clause concerns optional tenant contents insurance and does not require post-dated payments or restrict subletting or assignment, so neither candidate rule applies.
The clause imposes an absolute ban on assignment or subletting, contrary to ON-SUBLET-CONSENT, which requires consent not to be arbitrarily or unreasonably withheld.
The clause concerns the landlord’s repair and habitability obligations and does not authorize interference with vital services or entry without proper notice. Neither candidate rule is violated.
The clause violates ON-VITAL-SERVICES because it permits the landlord to shut off electricity, heat, or water due to late rent.
The clause allows the landlord to change the locks without giving the tenant new keys, which directly violates ON-LOCK-CHANGE, even when rent is in arrears.
The clause concerns smoking and does not prohibit pets or address consent to subletting or assignment, so neither candidate rule applies.

The clause only makes the tenant responsible for establishing and paying their electricity account; it neither permits interference with vital services nor requires post-dated cheques or automatic payments.
This severability clause does not prohibit pets or authorize interference with vital services, so neither candidate rule is violated.
